title: Golden Crumbly Polish Runner Beans Casserole
description: A traditional Polish dish featuring fermented runner beans, perfect for a post-workout meal, with a crumbly topping and a dash of friendly spices. This casserole is a hearty and satisfying option for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Poach the runner beans in boiling water for 5 minutes, then drain and set aside.
In a large bowl, mix together the sour cream, shredded cheese, and egg.
Add the poached runner beans to the bowl and stir until they are well coated with the cheese mixture.
In a separate bowl, mix together the breadcrumbs, salt, black pepper, and paprika.
Transfer the bean mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish and top with the breadcrumb mixture.
Bake the casserole in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
